what_it_does: This file is a generated surface specification for view C2 in area C; it records the surface title, platforms, represented concepts, journey membership (home journey J3 plus J5), the ui:// addresses it must build to, and the components, lint command, and token source used to build it.
observations: Status is 'planned' even though concrete build targets are listed.
Home journey is J3, and J5 appears in both journeys and cross_journeys.
Addresses use a ui:// scheme scoped to the components grow, inbox, and walkthrough.
The address list includes approval/rejection endpoints, course-of-action/resolve endpoints, and reason/verdict endpoints.
build_to embeds a design lint command, a token source path, and three component names.
The directory path mirrors the home_journey (surfaces/J3/C2.json).
The spec notes it was built by a Claude Code session and forbids invented addresses.
standouts: The address list mixes action endpoints (approve/reject/verdict) with concept endpoints (coa/resolve/reason).
J5 is both a journey and a cross_journey, signaling the surface spans journeys.
build_to couples the surface to a lint gate and a single token-derived CSS source.
The spec explicitly forbids inventing addresses, treating the address list as an authoritative reference.
conventions: Follows a structured surface-spec schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, home_journey, journeys, cross_journeys, addresses, and build_to.
Uses a directory layout that encodes the journey spine (tree) and the surface id.
Uses ui:// prefixed addresses scoped to components.
Uses escaped Unicode arrows in string values.
Breaks pure metadata convention by embedding build commands and token pipeline details in the same file.
concerns: The design_lint value contains a placeholder-like parenthetical, '(the FORM gate)', so it is not a runnable command.
The tokens value describes a transformation ('tokens.json -> design-system.css') rather than a concrete dependency reference.
Status is 'planned' while build_to already specifies lint and token targets, which may be aspirational.
walkthrough/verdict/approve is nested under walkthrough/verdict without explaining whether it is a sub-state or separate address.
notes: A planned surface spec that anchors J3's decision/value-choice view to concrete ui:// addresses and build-time constraints.
questions: What distinguishes a cross_journey from a regular journey entry?
How are ui:// addresses validated so that none are invented?
What is 'the FORM gate' in the lint command?
Why is J5 listed in both journeys and cross_journeys?
What do the represented codes INB-coa, INB-resolve, and GOV-resolve map to in the domain model?
Is walkthrough/verdict/approve a child of walkthrough/verdict or a separate endpoint?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the content: it is a generated surface specification that records journeys, addresses, and build targets for view C2.
intention_signals: The file tries to make a UI surface discoverable and buildable by binding it to stable journey references, concrete component addresses, and a single design-token/lint pipeline.
novelty: The use of a ui:// address scheme for UI capabilities and the embedding of lint/token build instructions inside a surface metadata spec.
contribution: It contributes a structured, journey-anchored mapping from a surface to the UI addresses, components, lint rule, and token source that would realize it.
summary_for_embedding: A planned generated surface specification for view C2 in area C, home journey J3, linking decision/value-choice UI addresses in grow, inbox, and walkthrough components to a lint command and token-derived CSS source.
intention_for_embedding: To record the journeys, cross-journey references, UI addresses, and build constraints needed to implement the C2 decision/value-choice surface.
suggested_fields: address_kind to tag each ui:// address as action, view, or state
journey_role to distinguish primary vs secondary journey membership
build_stage to separate planned, linted, tokenized, and implemented states
domain_concept_map to explain represented codes like INB-coa
